sentence_reviews.text: Limited, noisy, heuristic, or indirect labels are combined with unlabeled data.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Reviewed as synthesis across cited source pointers and, where applicable, the canonical atlas graph context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Former ambiguity resolved by narrowing the audit interpretation to synthesis support rather than requiring exact-source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Semi-supervised & weak supervision follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.2 'Supervised Learning' (line 493) and Chapter 19.6 'Learning with Less Data' (lines 512-516).
sentence_reviews.notes: Semi-supervised/weak supervision uses supervised label information, but the source does not state this combined category exactly.

sentence_reviews.text: Semi-supervised & weak supervision follows the learning setup expressed by Unsupervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.6 'Learning with Less Data' (lines 512-516) and Chapter 19.8 'Unsupervised Learning' (lines 523-532).
sentence_reviews.notes: Use of unlabeled data makes the unsupervised-learning connection defensible, but the exact rationale is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Semi-supervised & weak supervision is modeled as depending on Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning and feeding downstream nodes such as Medical imaging & clinical decision support, Computer vision, perception & scene understanding.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.

edge_reviews.notes: The relation to supervised learning is defensible because labels remain part of the setup; the combined weak-supervision category is not directly sourced.
The relation to unsupervised learning is defensible because unlabeled data is part of semi-supervised setups.

edge_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical dependency edge with relationship type, strength, direction, and rationale.
edge_reviews.notes: This verifies the explicit internal graph assertion. External source support for the conceptual dependency remains represented by node references and non-internal review records.
